description: Use with POST /admin/push passing notification_type=GOOGLE_UPDATE. Google pushes GOOGLE_UPDATE when they algorithmically change a business name, hours, or category. Your webhook handler must route on topicType and not crash when it receives something other than NEW_REVIEW. The location has realistic data so a GOOGLE_UPDATE might plausibly change its hours or category fields.

profile.description: Houston Heights' favourite gastropub. 48 craft beers on tap, Texan-sourced kitchen, and the best patio in the city. Game day HQ.

200000001.name: accounts/100000001/locations/200000001/reviews/rev001
200000001.comment: 48 taps and every single one is a good choice — the staff know the menu cold and make recommendations that are always spot-on. The smash burger is criminally underrated.

reviewReply.comment: Aaron, 48 taps is a menu and a mission. We train our staff hard on the pours. The smash burger appreciates the love! 🍔

200000001.name: accounts/100000001/locations/200000001/reviews/rev002
200000001.comment: Great patio bar for a Houston evening. Cooling misters actually work! Live music on Friday nights is a big draw. Beer selection is extraordinary.

200000001.name: accounts/100000001/locations/200000001/reviews/rev003
200000001.comment: Came for the Texans game and stayed for 4 hours. Multiple screens at every angle, great sound system, friendly bar staff who kept the food coming during commercials. Perfect sports bar.

reviewReply.comment: Carlos, game day at the Anchor is an experience we've worked hard to perfect! See you for the next game. 🏈

200000001.name: accounts/100000001/locations/200000001/reviews/rev004
200000001.comment: Good bar, mediocre food. The beer is excellent but the kitchen feels like an afterthought. The wings were lukewarm and the brisket sandwich was dry. Stick to apps and pints.

reviewReply.comment: Dana, the kitchen critique is fair — we're retooling the menu with a new exec chef joining in July. We think you'll be surprised. Come back this summer.

200000001.name: accounts/100000001/locations/200000001/reviews/rev005
200000001.comment: The DIPA from a local Heights brewery was flawless on nitro. Manager clearly curates the tap list with real care — nothing generic, all local or regional. This is how a craft bar should run.

reviewReply.comment: Eli, the tap list gets revised weekly and we'll never settle for macro beers. Cheers to good taste! 🍺

200000001.name: accounts/100000001/locations/200000001/reviews/rev006
200000001.comment: Solid Sunday brunch option. The Bloody Mary bar is a great concept and the brisket hash was well-executed. Patio was a bit hot by 11am but that's Houston.

200000001.name: accounts/100000001/locations/200000001/reviews/rev007
200000001.comment: The bar got absurdly loud on a Saturday night — couldn't hear the friend next to me, let alone order properly. The vibe is fun but the decibel level is a problem for anyone not 25.

reviewReply.comment: Greg, the Saturday decibels are a valid trade-off we wrestle with. We're looking at acoustic panels for the main room that won't kill the energy.

200000001.name: accounts/100000001/locations/200000001/reviews/rev008
200000001.comment: Trivia night on Wednesday is the best in Houston. Well-organized, good categories, fun host. The bar fills up so show up early to get a table. The jalapeño cream cheese pretzel is addictive.

reviewReply.comment: Helen, trivia night is one of our favourite nights of the week! The pretzel situation is a known problem — we're considering making them unlimited. 🥨
